_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- be accountable for digital, data and technology resilience across the organisation
- advocate for the appropriate level of investment to support organisational resilience 
- ensure that risks, including cyber security, privacy and compliance risks, are addressed by governance, operational processes, delivery practices and the strategic selection of suppliers
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- directing the digital, data and technology environment so the organisation can safely and securely harness innovative practices and emerging technologies
- working with the board to agree acceptable levels of information and cyber security risk, controls and mitigation strategies
- working with senior leaders to ensure that digital, data and technology is operating within organisational and external security governance frameworks
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifying processing activities that present high risk to individuals, and recommending proportionate technical and organizational measures to mitigate identified risks.
comments: DDaT skill superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-004 (DG, cosine: 0.538), DQ-S-009 (DQ, cosine: 0.531).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.602). Shared vocabulary: [data, risk, risks, skill]. DDaT-only terms [acceptable, accountable, across, addressed, advocate, agree] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, assessments, conducting, high, identified, identifying] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.833 (P=0.816, R=0.851). R exceeds P by 0.036 — the WIDAI KSA's 29 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 118 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (97.9%). "You can:
- be accountable for digital, data and technology resilience across the" and "Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifyin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.538 < STS 0.602 (gap -0.063). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [acceptable, accountable, across] and [activities, assessments, conducting] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.7%. Shared tokens: [data, risk, risks, skill]. DDaT-only: [acceptable, accountable, across, addressed, advocate, agree]. WIDAI-only: [activities, assessments, conducting, high, identified, identifying].
